codeReview.summary: This fix commit directly addresses the prior blocking failure from m3-indexing-reliability-recovery-and-requeue by adding deterministic id-based tie-breakers to all offset-paginated query paths involved in rebuild/re-embed/gap-repair traversal. Compared with the original failing commit (66924a8), the previously non-deterministic ORDER BY clauses now include id ASC in ConversationStore, SearchIndexStore (both offset overloads), and ArtifactStore, which removes row-order ambiguity under timestamp ties.

issues.description: The new rebuild/re-embed/gap-repair tie-collision tests use fixture sizes below the production page size (1000), so those specific flow tests do not traverse multiple pages. Multi-page behavior is exercised by test_offsetPagination_traversalYieldsCompleteSet_noSkipsNoDuplicates, but only for conversation pagination. Consider adding >1000 fixtures (or injectable page-size seams) for search document/artifact pagination to harden boundary coverage.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, prior failed review, and both diffs (66924a8 and 969e8c5). Deterministic pagination tie-breakers are now implemented on all offset query paths cited in the original blocking finding, so the fix adequately addresses the failure; remaining note is a non-blocking boundary-coverage gap in specific new tests.
